items.name: Orchestrator failure-row stabilization for 4-8 workers
items.degraded_mode: The orchestrator records precise failure reasons, poisoned-task thresholds, soft-success decisions, and original exit codes instead of collapsing failures into one generic status.
ready_when: Failure taxonomy and soft-success recovery behavior are covered by direct orchestrator unit fixtures.
not_ready_when: The row is treated as complete before direct try_soft_success_nonzero coverage lands.
acceptance: Failure rows emit a granular reason instead of contract_or_test_failure.
Non-timeout/non-OOM codex exits can become soft_success_nonzero only after final-report and commit verification pass.
Timeout and OOM exits remain hard failures.
items.note: Complete: failure rows now preserve granular verify/report reasons, poisoned-task records are covered, and soft-success recovery has direct unit coverage for timeout/OOM rejection, opt-out rejection, invalid-report rejection, dirty-commit rejection, valid report + clean commit recovery, and worker-state original_rc preservation via scripts/orchestrator/tests/unit/soft-success.bats.
done_signal: Fail-fast aborts terminate worker process trees and successful workers are still harvested before the failed cycle pauses.

items.name: Soft-success-nonzero bats coverage
items.degraded_mode: When the recovery guard refuses a non-zero exit, the worker state keeps the original exit reason and does not promote the run.
ready_when: Orchestrator failure-row stabilization remains active and try_soft_success_nonzero is testable without running the full worker loop.
not_ready_when: The slice attempts to revisit the already-decided ALLOW_SOFT_SUCCESS_NONZERO default.
acceptance: rc=124 and rc=137 always reject soft success.
ALLOW_SOFT_SUCCESS_NONZERO=0 rejects even with valid report and clean commit.
Valid report plus clean commit plus allowed non-zero rc records soft_success_nonzero with original_rc.
items.note: TDD landed: scripts/orchestrator/tests/unit/soft-success.bats source-loads the legacy entrypoint, directly covers try_soft_success_nonzero reject/recovery paths, and runs a one-worker fake-codexu success_nonzero fixture to prove worker state records mode=soft_success_nonzero with original_rc preserved. The default ALLOW_SOFT_SUCCESS_NONZERO remains on-by-default.
done_signal: Direct try_soft_success_nonzero cases cover reject and recovery paths with original_rc preserved.

items.name: OpenRouter
items.note: Not landed on main. Five codexu/* worker attempts on 2026-04-23 (`343ec085` / `c009045d` / `e7b271a7` / `f00010b1` / `b3b0c366`) produced candidate OpenRouter adapters but none merged — no `openrouter_*.go` file is tracked in `internal/hermes/` and `grep -i openrouter` over that package returns zero matches. Prior planner note incorrectly cited `agent/openrouter_client.py`, which does not exist upstream: `tools/openrouter_client.py` is only a 33-line async-client getter that delegates to `agent/auxiliary_client.py`. TDD scope: OpenRouter is not a standalone adapter upstream — it is one of several OpenAI-compatible providers routed through the shared provider resolver. Pin the behavior by porting against the real upstream surfaces: `agent/auxiliary_client.py:815 _try_openrouter`, `agent/auxiliary_client.py:2069 _is_openrouter_client`, the `X-OpenRouter-Title` / `X-OpenRouter-Categories` / `HTTP-Referer` app-attribution headers around `agent/auxiliary_client.py:170`, `agent/model_metadata.py:214 _is_openrouter_base_url`, `agent/usage_pricing.py:411 _openrouter_pricing_entry`, and `hermes_cli/runtime_provider.py:429 _resolve_openrouter_runtime`. Fixture-only unit tests must prove (a) base URL and model-family selection over the OpenAI-compatible client seam, (b) attribution-header emission, (c) retryable vs fatal error mapping through `internal/hermes/errors.go`, and (d) usage/pricing accounting against captured response fixtures — no live `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in tests. Land after `Provider interface + stream fixture harness` and `Tool-call normalization + continuation contract` closeout so this slice reuses the shared transcript harness rather than inventing its own.

items.name: Codex
items.note: Not landed on main. Four codexu/* worker attempts on 2026-04-23 (`a93551c9` / `a4c433f2` / `c9bc6d8d` / `a057f47c`) produced candidate Codex adapters but none merged — no `codex_*.go` file is tracked in `internal/hermes/` and `grep -i codex` over that package returns zero matches. TDD scope: upstream Codex is an OpenAI-Codex OAuth surface over the Responses API (not standard chat.completions), so the adapter must wrap `/v1/responses` at `https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex` — upstream surface lives at `agent/auxiliary_client.py:483 CodexAuxiliaryClient` / `:313 _CodexCompletionsAdapter` / `:501 _AsyncCodexCompletionsAdapter` / `:1038 _try_codex` (Responses→chat-completions shim, empty-output backfill from stream events, `gpt-5.2-codex` vs `gpt-5.3-codex` fallback), and the auth seam lives at `hermes_cli/auth.py:1451 refresh_codex_oauth_pure` / `:1546 _refresh_codex_auth_tokens` / `:1567 _import_codex_cli_tokens` / `:1601 resolve_codex_runtime_credentials` / `:3023 _codex_device_code_login`, with model enumeration at `hermes_cli/codex_models.py:147 get_codex_model_ids`. Pin behavior via fake-auth fixture tests: developer-message role handling, tool-call deltas, Responses→chat-completions envelope parity (including stream-event backfill when `response.output` arrives empty), `gpt-5.3-codex` rejection fallback, and explicit unsupported-feature errors. Upstream PR #12360 requires Codex auth state to live under Gormes's own home rather than touching `~/.codex/auth.json`, and upstream PR #2a2e5c0f requires 401/403 token-refresh failures to force a relogin rather than silently retrying with a stale token — freeze both behaviors as fake-auth unit tests before any live Codex request can fire. Land after `Provider interface + stream fixture harness`, `Tool-call normalization + continuation contract`, and `4.G Token vault` + `Multi-account auth` so the Codex adapter consumes a frozen credential seam rather than touching OS home paths directly.

items.name: Cross-provider reasoning-tag sanitization

Pure fixture-driven tests must cover closed `<think>...</think>` blocks, unterminated `<think>...` at stream EOF, and mixed reasoning-tag variants (`<think>`, `<reasoning>`, `<thought>`) without mutating the raw SSE transcript, and must separate (a) what is stored into assistant history, (b) what is rendered to operators, and (c) what `/resume`-style recap surfaces expose. Land before Codex/Gemini/OpenRouter/Bedrock bring new reasoning-model surfaces through the shared harness so each adapter inherits the same storage-vs-render behavior rather than reinventing it.

items.name: Per-turn model selection
items.note: Status reverted from `complete` after planner audit on 2026-04-24: the prior `complete` claim referenced commit `23b34cb5 feat(kernel): add per-turn model selection`, which only lives on `codexu/20260423T112611Z-3659166-014/worker4` and is NOT an ancestor of `main` (`git merge-base --is-ancestor 23b34cb5 HEAD` returns 1). On main today: `internal/kernel/frame.go:84` advertises a `PlatformEvent.Model` field with a doc-comment that references a `Config.ModelSelector` that does not exist; `internal/kernel/kernel.go:179` calls `runTurn(ctx, e.Text, e.SessionContext, e.CronJobID)` and silently drops `e.Model`; `internal/kernel/kernel.go:296` builds the `hermes.ChatRequest` with `Model: k.cfg.Model` (no per-turn override); `internal/kernel/model_selection.go` does not exist (`git cat-file -e HEAD:internal/kernel/model_selection.go` returns 128); and `grep -rn 'activeModel\|ModelSelector\|selectModel' internal/kernel` returns zero hits. Net: only the struct field landed; the wiring is dead. TDD (single small slice, no need to split): (a) add `internal/kernel/per_turn_model_test.go` (RED) that asserts a `PlatformEvent{Kind: PlatformEventSubmit, Model: "override-model"}` produces a `hermes.ChatRequest.Model == "override-model"` while `k.cfg.Model` remains the resident default for the next non-overriding event, and that the `RenderFrame.Model` reflects the per-turn choice for the duration of the turn; (b) thread `e.Model` through the `runTurn` signature into the `hermes.ChatRequest` build site at `internal/kernel/kernel.go:296` and into `emitFrame` at `internal/kernel/kernel.go:694`, falling back to `k.cfg.Model` when empty; (c) fix the misleading struct doc-comment at `internal/kernel/frame.go:84` to drop the `ModelSelector` reference until that companion 4.D registry/selector slice lands. Do NOT re-introduce `Config.ModelSelector` in this slice — that belongs in `Routing policy and fallback selector`. Keep `tm.SetModel` updates contained to the in-flight turn so telemetry per-turn vs default semantics are pinned by the same RED test.

items.name: Kernel retry honors Retry-After hint
items.note: Complete: TDD added `RetryBudget.NextDelayFor` so open-stream retry consumes the existing retry budget while preferring a provider `RetryAfter` hint when present, capping it at 16s, and falling back to the jittered schedule when absent. `internal/kernel/retry_test.go` covers capped hint behavior, fallback behavior, and a fast end-to-end retry through the kernel.

items.note: TDD: track upstream approvals.cron_mode drift by marking cron-fired turns as non-interactive and default-denying dangerous commands with an adaptive blocked message, while allowing an explicit approve mode to preserve old auto-approve behavior.

items.name: Tirith, path, URL, and website policy integration
items.note: TDD: compose external security findings with dangerous-command warnings into one guard decision so gateway/cron/CLI replays cannot bypass one check after only another was approved.

items.name: Sandboxed exec
items.note: TDD landed: Go-native `execute_code` now runs guarded `sh`/`python` snippets through a local sandbox contract with runtime selection, stdout/stderr caps, timeout status, and pre-exec filesystem/network blocking while broader backend-specific sandboxes remain Phase 5.B work.

items.name: Atomic checkpoints
items.note: TDD: port `tools/{file_operations,file_tools,checkpoint_manager,patch_parser}.py` as checkpoint-first read/write/patch primitives with rollback and path-policy fixtures before agent-visible writes.

items.name: Multi-model coordination
items.note: TDD: port `tools/mixture_of_agents_tool.py` only after provider routing and subagent envelopes are stable, using fake child model outputs to prove fan-out, aggregation, and failure isolation.

items.name: Todo
items.note: TDD: port `tools/todo_tool.py` as a small stateful tool with schema/result parity, deterministic persistence, and no dependency on the full file-tools port.

items.name: Clarify
items.note: TDD: port `tools/clarify_tool.py` as an interruptible prompt contract that preserves gateway/TUI response routing and times out safely in non-interactive cron turns.

items.name: Session search
items.note: TDD: port `tools/session_search_tool.py` on top of the Phase 3 session catalog, preserving same-chat defaults and opt-in user/source filters.

items.name: Debug helpers
items.note: TDD: port `tools/debug_helpers.py` as read-mostly diagnostic tools with redaction and no hidden writes before exposing them to production agents.

items.name: Cronjob tool API + schedule parser parity
items.note: TDD: port `tools/cronjob_tools.py` action/update/list surface plus `cron/jobs.py` once, interval, cron-expression, repeat-count, one-shot recovery, and stale recurring fast-forward behavior as fixtures over the Go cron store.

items.name: Cron prompt/script safety + pre-run script contract
items.note: TDD: port critical prompt threat scanning, invisible Unicode rejection, script-path confinement under the scripts directory, and configurable pre-run script timeout before exposing cron creation to tools.

items.name: Cron multi-target delivery + media/live-adapter fallback
items.note: TDD: port comma-separated deliver targets, home-channel/channel-directory resolution, response wrapping, MEDIA tag extraction, and live-adapter-first delivery fallback from `cron/scheduler.py` without changing the Phase 2.D MVP audit store.

items.name: 49-file CLI tree port
not_ready_when: The row is assigned as a whole hermes_cli tree migration instead of command-group slices.
unblocks: CLI command registry parity + active-turn busy policy
Config, profile, auth, and setup command surfaces
Gateway, platform, webhook, and cron management CLI
Diagnostics, backup, logs, and status CLI
items.note: Umbrella only: keep for inventory continuity, but execution should use smaller command/config/platform/diagnostic slices rather than attempting the whole hermes_cli tree at once.

items.name: Deterministic helper-file ports (banner/output/tips/webhook/dump)
items.note: TDD-first prerequisite slice for the larger 5.O command-group slices below. Port the pure-function helper surface from upstream hermes_cli as foundational primitives before any command-group or active-turn policy work lands on top: `hermes_cli/banner.py` -> `internal/cli/banner.go` (FormatContextLength, DisplayToolsetName, FormatBannerVersionLabel), `hermes_cli/cli_output.py` -> `internal/cli/output.go` (shared print/prompt helpers), `hermes_cli/tips.py` -> `internal/cli/tips.go` (tip corpus + GetRandomTip/PickRandomTip selector), `hermes_cli/webhook.py` -> `internal/cli/webhook.go` (deterministic helpers), `hermes_cli/dump.py` -> `internal/cli/dump.go` (deterministic helpers). Each helper file lands behind pure-function unit tests with fixed inputs/outputs — no terminal/TTY coupling, no config file reads, no time-of-day non-determinism. Evidence of granularity mismatch on main: as of planner run 2026-04-24, `git log --all --since='2026-04-23' --oneline | grep -cE 'feat\(cli\)|feat\(tuigateway\)'` reports **54 worker commits** across codexu/* branches attempting these helper ports with **zero** merged to main, and `internal/cli/` currently contains only `parity.go`/`root_test.go` — the larger command-group slices (`CLI command registry parity + active-turn busy policy`, `Config/profile/auth/setup`, `Gateway/platform/webhook/cron`, `Diagnostics/backup/logs/status`) are too big a jump from the current tree and must not consume the helper-file ports. Keep this slice strictly additive and pure-function — no command-registry wiring in this slice.

items.name: CLI command registry parity + active-turn busy policy
items.note: TDD: expand the Go command registry toward upstream hermes_cli/commands.py and prove every recognized gateway slash command either has a safe running-agent handler or returns a visible busy response without interrupting or queuing discardable command text.

items.name: Config, profile, auth, and setup command surfaces
items.note: TDD: port hermes_cli config/profile/auth/setup/uninstall flows as separate command groups with XDG-safe state paths and no implicit writes outside the selected Gormes home.

items.name: Gateway, platform, webhook, and cron management CLI
items.note: TDD: port operator management commands after the shared gateway read models land, so status/pairing/platform/cron commands consume the same stores as the runtime.

items.name: Diagnostics, backup, logs, and status CLI
items.note: TDD: port read-mostly diagnostics and backup surfaces independently from mutating setup/auth commands, with fixture-backed output tests before live filesystem integration.

items.name: OCI image
items.note: TDD: mirror upstream Docker entrypoint/config volume behavior with container smoke tests that run `gormes doctor --offline` and avoid bundling Python runtime paths.

items.name: Homebrew
items.note: TDD: add formula/release artifact checks after the static binary and docs install paths are stable, with version, checksum, and install layout fixtures.

items.name: Unix installer (install.sh) source-backed update flow
items.note: TDD slice 1 of installer parity (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-23-gormes-installer-parity.md`): port upstream Unix installer semantics into canonical `scripts/install.sh`, with the site embedding a synced `www.gormes.ai/internal/site/installers/install.sh` copy. The installer uses user-scoped PATH publication, a managed checkout under a Hermes-analogy install home, rerun-as-update behavior, and no root-owned target paths. Cover path selection, managed checkout, update-on-rerun, and publish+verify flow via a new `install_unix_test.go` Go test that executes the script under a temporary fake toolchain — no network calls in unit tests. Do not install Hermes/Python/Node: Gormes' current feature set does not require them.

items.name: Windows installer (install.ps1 + install.cmd) parity
items.note: TDD slice 2 of installer parity (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-23-gormes-installer-parity.md`): add `www.gormes.ai/internal/site/install.ps1` (native PowerShell) plus `install.cmd` wrapper that launches it, matching the Unix contract (user-scoped PATH, managed checkout, rerun-as-update, source-backed). Cover content/smoke assertions — PATH publication, managed checkout layout, release branch handling, and stable global command publication — via `install_windows_test.go` over fake toolchains; no live network calls in unit tests.

items.name: Installer site asset/route coverage
items.note: TDD slice 3 of installer parity (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-23-gormes-installer-parity.md`): extend `www.gormes.ai/internal/site/{assets,server,content,static_export}.go` and matching `assets_test.go`/`static_export_test.go` coverage so `/install.sh`, `/install.ps1`, and `/install.cmd` all embed, load, serve, and static-export with the correct MIME and cache behavior. Update landing-page install copy (`content.go` + `home.spec.mjs`) and the top-level `README.md` / `www.gormes.ai/README.md` install guidance only after the script slices land. This slice freezes the public install-surface contract so downstream docs/site builds do not regress.

items.name: Deterministic helper-file ports (tool-progress/image/completion-path/personality/platform-event)
items.note: TDD-first prerequisite slice for the `SSE streaming to Bubble Tea TUI` and API-server slices below. Port the pure-function helper surface from upstream `tui_gateway/` as foundational primitives before any transport/lifecycle work lands: `_load_tool_progress_mode` -> ParseToolProgressMode/ToolProgressEnabled, `_normalize_completion_path` -> NormalizeCompletionPath, `_estimate_image_tokens`/`_image_meta` -> EstimateImageTokens/ImageMeta/ReadImageMeta, upstream tool-progress helpers -> FormatToolDuration/CountList/ToolSummary, personality validator + renderer, and the upstream `PlatformEvent` struct surface for the remote Bubble Tea TUI. Each helper lands behind pure-function unit tests with fixed inputs/outputs — no HTTP, no SSE, no JSON-RPC dispatch, no Bubble Tea program in this slice. Evidence of granularity mismatch on main: as of planner run 2026-04-24, `internal/tuigateway/` does not exist in the tree, and `git log --all --since='2026-04-23'` shows **repeated worker attempts** on codexu/* branches for each of these helpers (tool-progress mode, completion-path, image primitives, tool-progress helpers, personality validator, PlatformEvent, RemoteClient, JSONRPCDispatcher, NewGatewayMux) with **zero** merged to main — confirming the `SSE streaming to Bubble Tea TUI` slice is too big a jump from the current tree and must not consume the helper-file ports. Keep this slice strictly additive and pure-function — no NewRemoteClient, no NewGatewayMux, no SSE dial in this slice; those land in the SSE-streaming slice once the primitives are green.

items.name: SSE streaming to Bubble Tea TUI
items.note: TDD: port the tui_gateway server/render/slash-worker event contract so remote Bubble Tea clients can subscribe to live agent state without the Python gateway process. Depends on the `Deterministic helper-file ports` slice above — this slice owns the transport/lifecycle wiring (DialSSE, NewRemoteClient, NewGatewayMux, JSONRPCDispatcher, PostPlatformEvent, `--remote <url>` threading through runTUI) that consumes the frozen helper primitives. Do not widen the helper-primitives contract here.

items.name: OpenAI-compatible chat-completions API server
items.note: TDD: port the upstream gateway/platforms/api_server.py /v1/chat/completions surface with API-key auth, body-size limits, OpenAI content-part normalization, X-Hermes-Session-Id continuity, and streaming/non-streaming error-envelope fixtures before exposing the native Go brain over HTTP.

items.name: Responses API store + run event stream
items.note: TDD: port ResponseStore as a bounded persistent read model, previous_response_id chaining, GET/DELETE /v1/responses, POST /v1/runs, GET /v1/runs/{run_id}/events SSE, and orphaned-run cleanup as fixtures before external OpenAI-compatible clients depend on stateful runs.

items.name: API server health + cron admin endpoints
items.note: TDD: port /health/detailed plus /api/jobs list/create/update/delete/pause/resume/trigger after the Phase 5.N cronjob store/API parity slices land, reusing the same auth and OpenAI-style error envelope instead of creating a second cron control plane.

items.name: Execution-mode resolver + config precedence
items.note: TDD slice 1 of 4: pure helper in `internal/tools/execute_code.go` that returns `off`/`project`/`strict` from (a) env var (e.g. `GORMES_EXECUTE_CODE_MODE`), then (b) config key, then (c) default, with unknown values falling back to default and emitting a warning. Table tests only — no sandbox coupling, no CWD/interpreter behavior yet. Freeze the token set here so later slices cannot silently add modes.

items.name: Strict-mode CWD + interpreter parity
items.note: TDD slice 2 of 4: strict mode keeps today's behavior frozen — isolated tmpdir CWD plus the canonical interpreter resolved via `LocalCodeSandbox.resolveRuntime`. Fixtures must prove the 5.K blocked-result envelope (status, error, filesystem_access, network_access) is unchanged and that relative imports/filesystem access outside the tmpdir continue to fail. This slice exists only to pin the reference behavior before project-mode lands.

items.name: Project-mode CWD + active venv detection
items.note: TDD slice 3 of 4: port upstream `_resolve_python_binary`/`_resolve_cwd` project semantics — session's working directory for CWD and the active virtualenv/conda python when present, falling back to the resolved system interpreter. Unit tests must drive resolution via injected env (`VIRTUAL_ENV`, `CONDA_PREFIX`), not the real host. Do not widen the 5.K filesystem/network blocking contract — project mode still flows through the same `sandboxGuardReason` gate.

items.name: Default mode selection + config cut-over
items.note: TDD slice 4 of 4: pick and document Gormes' default mode (recommend `strict` given the self-hosted security-first posture, with `project` opt-in via config). Wire the resolver into `NewExecuteCodeTool` so callers get the right mode without per-call arguments, add a regression test that the 5.K `Sandboxed exec` blocked-result envelope is byte-identical across modes, and update the rationale in `docs/content/building-gormes/core-systems/tool-execution.md` so operators understand the trade-off before flipping the default.

6.name: Phase 6 — The Learning Loop (Soul)
6.deliverable: Native skill extraction. Compounding intelligence. The feature Hermes doesn't have.

items.name: Heuristic or LLM-scored signal
items.note: TDD: start with deterministic local signals from transcript length, tool-call count, retries, edits, and operator feedback before adding any LLM scorer, so learning-loop triggers are explainable.

items.name: LLM-assisted pattern distillation
items.note: TDD: define the extraction input bundle and output schema with fake model fixtures, including rejection of secrets, one-off tasks, and implementation-specific noise before live generation.

items.name: Portable SKILL.md format
items.degraded_mode: Skill status excludes unreviewed or invalid drafts from prompt injection and records resolver or metadata failures.
items.fixture: internal/skills SKILL.md metadata, provenance, and review-state fixtures
ready_when: Phase 2.G skills runtime is complete and the parser/store seam is stable enough for versioned metadata.
not_ready_when: Generated drafts are allowed into prompt injection without explicit review metadata.
acceptance: SKILL.md metadata includes version, provenance, review state, triggers, and exclusions.
Invalid, disabled, or unreviewed drafts never enter prompt injection.
Atomic writes preserve human-editable skill files and version history.
items.note: TDD: extend the Phase 2.G parser/store contract with versioned metadata, provenance, review state, and atomic writes while preserving human-editable SKILL.md files.
done_signal: SKILL.md metadata fixtures prove invalid, disabled, or unreviewed drafts stay out of prompt injection.

items.name: Hybrid lexical + semantic lookup
items.note: TDD: combine lexical tags with Phase 3 semantic embeddings behind a deterministic scorer, proving disabled/unreviewed skills stay excluded from prompt injection.

items.name: Skill effectiveness scoring
items.note: TDD: persist skill-use outcomes, explicit operator feedback, and decay/weight adjustments as auditable records before any automatic promotion or demotion behavior.

items.name: TUI + Telegram browsing
items.note: TDD: expose browse/edit/disable/review flows through TUI and gateway commands after the store and feedback records are stable, with permission and active-turn behavior pinned by tests.
